title: Full PC Health Check
description: Run a broad read-only PC health review across system, storage, security, performance, and event evidence before recommending fixes.
starter_prompt: Perform a full PC health check. Start read-only and report findings before making changes.

phases.prompt: Define read-only diagnostic scope and identify any missing context.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; function Section($n){ Write-Output ('== ' + $n + ' ==') }; function TrySection($n,[scriptblock]$b){ Section $n; try { & $b } catch { Write-Output ('[warning] ' + $n + ' unavailable: ' + $_.Exception.Message) } }; TrySection 'System' { Get-ComputerInfo | Select-Object OsName,OsVersion,OsBuildNumber,WindowsVersion,CsManufacturer,CsModel,CsTotalPhysicalMemory | Format-List }; TrySection 'Uptime' { Get-CimInstance Win32_OperatingSystem | Select-Object LastBootUpTime,LocalDateTime | Format-List }; TrySection 'Volumes' { Get-Volume | Select-Object DriveLetter,FileSystemLabel,FileSystem,HealthStatus,OperationalStatus,SizeRemaining,Size | Format-List }; TrySection 'Physical disks' { Get-PhysicalDisk | Select-Object FriendlyName,SerialNumber,MediaType,HealthStatus,OperationalStatus,Size | Format-List }; TrySection 'Storage reliability' { $pd=Get-PhysicalDisk -ErrorAction Stop; try { $counters=$pd | Get-StorageReliabilityCounter -ErrorAction Stop; if($counters){ $counters | Select-Object DeviceId,Temperature,ReadErrorsTotal,WriteErrorsTotal,ReadErrorsUncorrected,WriteErrorsUncorrected,Wear,PowerOnHours,PowerCycleCount | Format-List } else { Write-Output '[warning] No storage reliability counters returned.' } } catch { Write-Output ('[warning] Storage reliability counters unavailable without administrator/storage CIM access: ' + $_.Exception.Message); Write-Output 'Fallback: physical disk, Win32_DiskDrive, Get-Disk, and event-log evidence collected below.' } }; TrySection 'Disk fallback status' { Get-CimInstance Win32_DiskDrive | Select-Object Model,SerialNumber,MediaType,Status,Size,InterfaceType | Format-List; Get-Disk -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object Number,FriendlyName,HealthStatus,OperationalStatus,PartitionStyle,Size,IsBoot,IsSystem | Format-List }; TrySection 'Top CPU live sample' { $s1=@{}; Get-Process | Where-Object { $null -ne $_.CPU } | ForEach-Object { $s1[$_.Id]=$_.CPU }; Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 1200; Get-Process | Where-Object { $null -ne $_.CPU -and $s1.ContainsKey($_.Id) } | ForEach-Object { [pscustomobject]@{ProcessName=$_.ProcessName;Id=$_.Id;CpuSecondsDelta=[math]::Round(($_.CPU-$s1[$_.Id]),3);WorkingSetMB=[math]::Round($_.WorkingSet64/1MB,1)} } | Sort-Object CpuSecondsDelta -Descending | Select-Object -First 10 | Format-Table -AutoSize }; TrySection 'Memory' { Get-CimInstance Win32_OperatingSystem | Select-Object FreePhysicalMemory,TotalVisibleMemorySize | Format-List }; TrySection 'Defender' { Get-MpComputerStatus -ErrorAction Stop | Select-Object AMServiceEnabled,AntivirusEnabled,RealTimeProtectionEnabled,AntivirusSignatureLastUpdated | Format-List }; TrySection 'Critical event summary' { $events=Get-WinEvent -FilterHashtable @{LogName='System'; Level=1,2; StartTime=(Get-Date).AddDays(-7)} -MaxEvents 200 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; if($events){ $events | Group-Object ProviderName,Id,LevelDisplayName | Sort-Object Count -Descending | Select-Object Count,Name | Format-Table -AutoSize; Write-Output '== Critical event details =='; $events | Select-Object -First 40 TimeCreated,ProviderName,Id,LevelDisplayName,Message | Format-List } else { Write-Output 'No critical/error system events found in the last 7 days.' } }

phases.prompt: Collect broad read-only health evidence from system, disk, memory, security, live process sample, and event-log sources. Treat any '[warning]' lines as missing evidence that must be disclosed.
phases.completion: Raw health evidence is captured and any missing admin-only evidence is explicitly visible.

phases.prompt: Analyze collected OS/build, uptime, disk health, storage reliability or fallback disk evidence, memory pressure, live CPU sample, Defender state, and critical event log evidence. Call out any missing evidence caused by permission limits.
phases.completion: Evidence is grouped by system area.

phases.prompt: Prioritize findings by user impact, safety, and confidence. Distinguish current performance signals from lifetime counters.
phases.completion: Findings have severity and recommended next steps.

phases.prompt: Check whether evidence supports the findings and whether any high-value read-only check is missing. Verify that permission-denied storage counters are not presented as healthy evidence.
phases.completion: Review passes or gaps are listed.

phases.prompt: Create a technician and customer-ready health report with evidence, findings, actions/tools used, verification status, artifacts/logs, cleanup status, residual risk, and prioritized next actions. Include missing-evidence warnings when any command section reported '[warning]'.
Keep logs and reports, remove only temp files created by this workflow.

acceptance_criteria: Read-only diagnostics ran before any repair recommendation.
Disk, event log, security, performance, and reliability evidence were considered.
Any permission-denied or missing admin-only evidence is explicitly disclosed.
CPU/process findings use a live sample rather than lifetime CPU totals.
Findings include severity, confidence, evidence, and next step.
Final response states no changes were made unless the user approved them.
